plan.summary: A 4-day solo food-focused trip to Tokyo exploring diverse culinary experiences from street food to traditional dining, combined with cultural visits to enhance the travel experience. Budget-conscious with efficient transportation and comfortable accommodation.

activities: Visit Tsukiji Outer Market for fresh seafood and street food tasting
Explore Ginza district for shopping and cafes
Evening walk at Shibuya Crossing and visit nearby izakayas
food: Breakfast at Tsukiji Outer Market stalls
Lunch at a sushi restaurant in Ginza
Dinner at a popular izakaya in Shibuya
daily_plan.transport: Use Tokyo Metro and JR lines with a prepaid IC card (Suica or Pasmo)

activities: Morning visit to Asakusa and Senso-ji Temple
Explore Nakamise Shopping Street for traditional snacks
Afternoon visit to Ueno Park and Ameya-Yokocho market
food: Breakfast at a local café in Asakusa
Lunch trying tempura at a local restaurant
Dinner at a ramen shop near Ueno
daily_plan.transport: Tokyo Metro and local buses using IC card

activities: Day trip to Tsukiji Fish Market for sushi breakfast
Visit teamLab Borderless digital art museum in Odaiba
Evening stroll along Odaiba waterfront
food: Dinner at a yakitori restaurant in Odaiba
daily_plan.transport: JR Yamanote Line and Yurikamome Line with IC card

activities: Visit Harajuku for trendy cafes and crepes
Walk through Meiji Shrine
Last minute shopping in Omotesando
food: Breakfast at a Harajuku crepe stand
Lunch at a café in Omotesando
Dinner at a traditional Japanese restaurant

hotel_recommendations: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku - central location, good for solo travelers
Tokyu Stay Shibuya - convenient for food and nightlife
APA Hotel Tokyo-Shiomi-Ekimae - budget-friendly with easy metro access
transportation_overview: Tokyo Metro and JR lines are the most efficient for city travel
Purchase a Suica or Pasmo IC card for convenient tap-in/out on trains and buses
Walking is recommended in concentrated areas like Harajuku and Shibuya
Yurikamome Line for Odaiba area access

cost_breakdown.accommodation: ¥20000 (approx. $150 USD) for 3 nights in a mid-range hotel
cost_breakdown.food: ¥28000 (approx. $210 USD) covering all meals and snacks
cost_breakdown.transport: ¥4000 (approx. $30 USD) for IC card top-ups and day trips
cost_breakdown.activities: ¥3000 (approx. $23 USD) for entrance fees and small purchases

travel_tips: Carry a prepaid IC card for easy transportation
Learn basic Japanese greetings and food-related phrases
Cash is preferred in many small eateries; ATMs are widely available
Try to avoid peak commuting hours on trains for comfort
Respect local customs especially in religious sites
safety_notes: Tokyo is generally very safe but keep an eye on personal belongings in crowded areas
Follow local COVID-19 guidelines and hygiene practices
Use well-lit and populated routes when walking at night
Keep emergency contact numbers and embassy info handy
Be cautious when trying unfamiliar foods if you have allergies

plan.summary: An 8-day solo food-focused trip to Thailand from February 20 to 27, 2026, exploring Bangkok and Chiang Mai's vibrant street food scenes, local markets, and cooking classes within a budget of 50,000 THB.

activities: Arrival in Bangkok
Check-in at hotel
Evening walk and street food tasting at Yaowarat (Chinatown)
daily_plan.transport: Airport taxi to hotel, walking in Chinatown

activities: Visit Or Tor Kor Market
Lunch at local market stalls
Explore Chatuchak Weekend Market (if weekend)
Evening dinner at a popular local Thai restaurant
food: Fresh fruit and Thai dishes at Or Tor Kor Market

activities: Day tour to Ayutthaya with food stops
Try local river prawns and Thai desserts
Return to Bangkok
daily_plan.transport: Tour van or train to Ayutthaya

activities: Flight to Chiang Mai
Check-in at hotel
Evening visit to Chiang Mai Night Bazaar
food: Khao Soi (Northern Thai curry noodle soup)
daily_plan.transport: Domestic flight, taxi to hotel, walking

activities: Morning Thai cooking class
Visit Warorot Market
Explore local cafes
Day trip to Doi Suthep and local villages
Try local hill tribe food specialties
Relax at hotel or spa
Explore Nimmanhaemin Road food scene
Dinner at a well-reviewed local restaurant
Morning local market visit
Flight back to Bangkok and onward departure

hotel_recommendations: Bangkok: Rambuttri Village Inn & Plaza (budget-friendly, central location)
Chiang Mai: Green Tiger Vegetarian House (comfortable, near Night Bazaar)
local_food_spots: Nimmanhaemin Road food stalls, Chiang Mai
transportation_overview: Use airport taxis or Grab app for airport transfers
BTS Skytrain and MRT in Bangkok for city travel
Songthaews and taxis in Chiang Mai
Domestic flights between Bangkok and Chiang Mai
Walking for local market and street food exploration
packing_list: Lightweight clothing suitable for warm weather
Travel umbrella or raincoat (February is dry but occasional showers possible)
Power adapter (Type A, B, C, F plugs used in Thailand)
travel_tips: Carry small change for street food and market purchases
Use reputable taxi services or ride-hailing apps for safety
Respect local customs and dress modestly when visiting temples
Learn basic Thai greetings to enhance interactions
Keep an eye on belongings in crowded markets
safety_notes: Avoid drinking tap water; use bottled water
Be cautious when crossing streets; traffic can be busy and chaotic
Beware of scams around tourist areas; agree on taxi fares beforehand
Keep valuables secure and avoid displaying expensive items
Follow local COVID-19 guidelines and health advisories
Inform someone of your daily itinerary when traveling solo
